item.title: Tennis Ball Retrieval Robot
item.description: An autonomous mecanum-wheel robot that detects, chases, and retrieves tennis balls on a court using dual cameras, a custom YOLO model, and a servo-controlled claw.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.body: An autonomous robot built to retrieve tennis balls on a court and return them to a designated drop zone. The system uses a mecanum-wheel chassis for omnidirectional movement, a servo-controlled claw for ball pickup, and a dual-camera perception stack split across a Jetson Orin Nano (vision) and a Raspberry Pi (motion control) communicating over ROS2.

sections.heading: Hardware
bullets: Mecanum-wheel chassis driven by 4 brushless motors via ODrive ESCs on CAN bus
ZED 2i stereo camera for far-range 3D ball detection and visual odometry
Intel RealSense D435i RGBD camera for close-range detection and blind-spot handling
2-DOF servo arm (Dynamixel) with a claw for grasping and releasing tennis balls
Jetson Orin Nano running the perception stack; Raspberry Pi running motion control

sections.heading: Perception Pipeline
sections.body: A custom YOLOv8 model (trained on court footage) runs on the Jetson and detects three classes simultaneously: tennis ball, drop-zone water bottle, and court net strap. The ZED 2i handles far-range detection using the ZED SDK's built-in AI object detection. The RealSense takes over at close range, with a fallback that estimates z=0.10m when depth returns NaN due to the camera blind spot.

sections.heading: Autonomous Mission
steps: INIT: Lock the drop-zone bottle position via TF, rotate to face the court.
WAITING: Monitor ball stillness — chase only begins after the ball is stationary for 3 seconds.
CHASING (ZED): Proportional angular + fixed linear drive toward ball using ZED far-range detection.
CHASING (RealSense): Handoff to close-range camera; arm tilts down as distance decreases.
GRABBING: Arm sweeps down, claw closes, arm raises.
RETURN: Navigate back to bottle drop zone using odometry and visual re-acquisition.
DROPPING: Arm sweeps down, claw opens, arm raises. Repeat mission.

sections.heading: Key Technical Challenges
bullets: Camera handoff: Seamless transition between ZED (far) and RealSense (close) to handle each camera's blind spot.
Ball stillness detection: Prevents chasing rolling balls by requiring 3s of positional stability within a 0.1m × 0.4m window.
Drop zone exclusion: Logs dropped ball positions to avoid re-chasing just-released balls.
ODrive initialization: Robust retry logic ensures all 4 motor axes enter closed-loop control before accepting commands.
Distributed compute: Vision runs on Jetson GPU, control runs on Pi — coordinated purely through ROS2 topics over the network.
